mission.title: Install and Configure RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
mission.description: The RabbitMQ Cluster Operator is a Kubernetes operator designed to deploy and manage RabbitMQ clusters seamlessly. It automates the lifecycle management of RabbitMQ instances, making it easier to scale and maintain clusters. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add rabbitmq https://charts.rabbitmq.com

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the RabbitMQ Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator using Helm, which manages the deployment of RabbitMQ clusters.

```bash

helm install rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --version 2.19.2 --namespace rabbitmq-system --create-namespace

```

> Installs the operator in a dedicated namespace.

steps.title: Deploy a RabbitMQ cluster
steps.description:
This step deploys a sample RabbitMQ cluster using a predefined configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rabbitmq/cluster-operator/main/docs/examples/hello-world/rabbitmq.yaml

```

> Deploys a sample RabbitMQ cluster.

steps.title: Verify the RabbitMQ cluster deployment
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the RabbitMQ pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq-system

kubectl get rabbitmqclusters -n rabbitmq-system

```

> Lists all pods in the rabbitmq-system namespace.
> Lists all RabbitMQ clusters in the namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if persistent volumes are not handled properly.

```bash

helm uninstall rabbitmq-operator --namespace rabbitmq-system

kubectl delete namespace rabbitmq-system

```

> Uninstalls the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your RabbitMQ data. This step upgrades the operator to the specified version and verifies the deployment.

```bash

kubectl get rabbitmqclusters -n rabbitmq-system -o yaml > backup-rabbitmqclusters.yaml

helm upgrade rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --version 2.19.2 --namespace rabbitmq-system

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq-system

```

> Backs up the current RabbitMQ cluster configuration.
> Upgrades the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator.
> Verifies that the RabbitMQ pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get rabbitmqclusters -n rabbitmq-system -o yaml > backup-rabbitmqclusters.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create RabbitMQ cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient permissions or misconfigured RBAC settings.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the operator has the necessary permissions and that RBAC is configured correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the RabbitMQ configuration is incorrect or if there are resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n rabbitmq-system' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: RabbitMQ cluster is not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Network policies or service configurations may be blocking access.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configurations and network policies to ensure proper access.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources to schedule pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources available.

**Fix:**
Scale up your Kubernetes nodes or adjust resource requests/limits in your RabbitMQ configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Improved handling of RabbitMQ cluster upgrades and added support for custom resource definitions.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations noted in this release.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the RabbitMQ pods in a 'Running' state when executing 'kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq-system'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster with kubectl and Helm installed.
